title: Why does Asukas mood swing so rapidly in episode 9
display_title: Why does Asukas mood swing so rapidly in episode 9
wikitext:
[]: Why does Asuka's mood swing so rapidly in episode 9             Quote Post by Naz » Mon Sep 24, 2007 6:01 pm I'm sure this has been brought up before, but I looked all over and just CAN'T find it :( In episode 9 after all the training is completed for the DDR fight, Asuka and Shinji are alone at home. Asuka walks by and happily makes the peace fingers mentioning "We're all alone tonight then". Suddenly, the next clip with Asuka involves her going into her room and telling Shinji not to come in or she'll kill him. I KNOW I read somewhere someone talking about how they intentionally left out a scene here leaving us to figure out what happened between the two to make her mood change so violently from happy to mad. My question is, WHAT HAPPENED THAT THEY LEFT OUT!?! Or better yet, who has a link to the discussion about this topic that I am sure already happened somewhere. Sorry for posting this again if it really has been discussed.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Sep 24, 2007 6:05 pm Shinji sitting there like a completely lost twit. She was pretty much offering herself to him and he just sat there. See the NPC of episode 22, Asuka is sitting behind the door thinking something along the lines of "You wont help me" after the Wall of Jerico bit.

[]:             Quote Post by Incisivis » Mon Sep 24, 2007 6:12 pm Adding another vote for a "reverse psychology". But given the way Asuka had been treating Shinji, I don't blame him for not getting it.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Mon Sep 24, 2007 6:37 pm Asuka likes attention Shinji wasn't geving her any or at least the attention she wanted. Shinji had a pretty girl around and had no idea how to ahem return her affection :P

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Mon Sep 24, 2007 6:41 pm What everyone else said. The guy just wouldn't make a move, and she was, ahem, "disappointed", to say the least.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Sep 24, 2007 9:56 pm You know, I don't get it. People around here can make masturbation jokes inspired by the begining of EoE but they can't just come out and say Asuka wanted sex.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Mon Sep 24, 2007 9:57 pm Asuka wanted sex.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Sep 24, 2007 10:36 pm Sex? I doubt it. I think she wanted Shinji to make an advance, which she would then proceed to reject.

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Mon Sep 24, 2007 10:37 pm Asuka had an intense...emotional desire for Shinji. She dropped hints for a potential union, with such knowledge failing to be recognized by Shinji. This swaying gesture, hanging in the air for several moments, finally crashed with Asuka's disappointment of Shinji's lack of a "hint" detector.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Mon Sep 24, 2007 10:40 pm NAveryW wrote: Sex? I doubt it. I think she wanted Shinji to make an advance, which she would then proceed to reject.

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Mon Sep 24, 2007 10:44 pm chee wrote: I was responding to Anonymous Evafan in a sort of jokey manner.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Mon Sep 24, 2007 11:14 pm maybe it would be better to state that Asuka wanted the sexual tension to be "relieved"?

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Mon Sep 24, 2007 11:38 pm I suppose so; why not.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Sep 24, 2007 11:42 pm Note that she calls the closed door the "Walls of Jericho" Hebrews 11:30;31 wrote: By faith the walls of Jericho fell down, after they were compassed for seven days. By faith the harlot Rahab perished not with them that believed not, when she had received the spies with peace.

[]: Re: Why does Asuka's mood swing so rapidly in episode 9             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Sep 25, 2007 1:16 am Naz wrote: I'm sure this has been brought up before, but I looked all over and just CAN'T find it :(

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Tue Sep 25, 2007 5:04 am Girls want attention, especially from the guy they are attracted to. If they don't get it, they become mad in front of the guy--suggesting that the whole speech "make a move" was a joke-- while deep down they get hurt. I don't think Asuka wanted sex, just some attention. It hurts her pride that she didn't get her.

[]:             Quote Post by omega_cc » Tue Sep 25, 2007 8:37 am I agree with chee, that she wanted affection, but didn't they kiss in a certain episode, which caused Asuka to immediately wash out her mouth because it was nasty? BTW, Shinji is a failure to men everywhere. Hot chick in his apartment and he listens to music instead of hitting on her. Even if shes a bitch.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Tue Sep 25, 2007 9:35 am omega_cc wrote: I agree with chee, that she wanted affection, but didn't they kiss in a certain episode, which caused Asuka to immediately wash out her mouth because it was nasty? BTW, Shinji is a failure to men everywhere. Hot chick in his apartment and he listens to music instead of hitting on her. Even if shes a bitch.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Sep 25, 2007 12:10 pm the_seventh_child wrote: I don't think Asuka wanted sex, just some attention.

[]:             Quote Post by Naz » Tue Sep 25, 2007 4:00 pm Thanks everyone. I actually had that general idea, but I thought there was also some sort of crazy plot development going on at the same time haha; probably just the way I kept thinking, "they left something out here...." Still, good stuff. Thanks :)
